entities: BOUNDARIES: TOWARDS A TRANSFORMATIVE HERMENEUTICS OF QUANTUM GRAVITY
WAR OF THE WORLDS RADIO BROADCAST
relations: The Piltdown Man hoax misled the scientific community for decades, sparking debate and skepticism about evolutionary theory
Both the Piltdown Man and the War of the Worlds broadcast were hoaxes that challenged established beliefs and tested the boundaries of truth and fiction
George Hull created the Cardiff Giant as a hoax
The Cardiff Giant hoax captivated the public and attracted massive crowds before its true nature was uncovered
The War of the Worlds radio broadcast caused panic among the public, demonstrating the influence of media on perception
WAR OF THE WORLDS RADIO BROADCAST
Orson Welles directed and narrated the War of the Worlds broadcast that caused mass hysteria
WAR OF THE WORLDS RADIO BROADCAST
The War of the Worlds broadcast was an adaptation of H.G. Wells' novel
Hermann Göring purchased a forged Vermeer from Han van Meegeren
Alan Sokal was the physicist behind the Sokal Affair, submitting a fake paper to critique postmodernism
The Sokal Affair was sparked by Social Text's publication of a nonsensical article, leading to a debate on academic credibility
Alan Sokal submitted a fake paper to Social Text, which was published, as part of the Sokal Affair
The article was published by Social Text, leading to the Sokal Affair and debates on postmodernist scholarship credibility
